# Project Charta

## Context and Scope
This project operates within the domain of Swiss politics and civic tech, focusing on the parliamentary debates of the Swiss Federal Assembly. The project addresses the semantic landscape of political speeches. The core question it seeks to answer is: *Are Swiss political parties truly polarized in their rhetoric, or do they use similar language and semantic structures when discussing key issues?* The expected benefit is to provide transparency and an intuitive, visual way for the public and media to understand the ideological proximity of political actors without having to read thousands of pages of official transcripts. The final visualization product will be published as an interactive online dashboard (e.g., Streamlit).

## Project Objectives and Success Criteria
**Project Objectives (Client/Stakeholder Perspective):**

* Enable users to visually explore the ideological and semantic similarities between Swiss politicians and parties based on their official speeches.
* Provide an interactive tool to filter parliamentary speeches by Party, Canton, and Language.
* Deliver an intuitive interface that translates complex AI-generated text embeddings into an easily readable format for non-technical users.

**Success Criteria:**

* **Qualitative:** Users can successfully and quickly identify thematic clusters (e.g., whether members of the SP and the Greens cluster together semantically compared to the SVP).
* **Quantitative:** The dataset must contain at least 100 observations (speeches) and 6 features (including UMAP coordinates, Word Count, Party, etc.). The dashboard must load and render the interactive scatter plot smoothly.

**Out of Scope:** Real-time live transcription analysis, audio processing of speeches, and predictive modeling (e.g., predicting how a politician will vote).

## Stakeholder Analysis
* **Project Team (3 Students):** Responsible for data engineering, AI processing, and visualization design. Goal: Successfully complete the module requirements.
* **Course Instructors:** Evaluate the project based on reproducibility, technical implementation, and storytelling quality.
* **Political Journalists:** Professional end-users looking for data-driven insights and quantitative proof of political polarization.
* **Voters / Engaged Citizens:** General end-users seeking a transparent, big-picture overview of Swiss political dynamics.

## User Analysis
We have identified two primary personas based on the Value Proposition Canvas [@Osterwalder2014]:

### Persona 1: The Political Journalist (e.g., Sarah, 35)
* **Attributes:** High domain expertise in Swiss politics, medium data literacy, primarily uses a desktop computer, needs to use the tool weekly for research.
* **User tasks (Jobs):** Writing data-driven articles on political polarization; finding specific quotes or identifying which politicians are driving a specific narrative.
* **Pains:** Reading through thousands of pages of the *Amtliches Bulletin* (official transcripts) is incredibly time-consuming. It is difficult to prove "polarization" quantitatively rather than just anecdotally.
* **Gains:** A tool that instantly highlights outliers (e.g., a politician speaking more like an opposing party) to formulate compelling journalistic narratives.

### Persona 2: The Engaged Citizen (e.g., Marco, 42)
* **Attributes:** Medium domain expertise, low data literacy, primarily uses mobile/tablet, occasional usage (usually around elections).
* **User tasks (Jobs):** Deciding who to vote for; trying to understand if a party actually aligns with their personal values in daily parliamentary business.
* **Pains:** Political rhetoric is dense, dry, and hard to follow. It is difficult to see the "big picture" of what a party actually focuses on.
* **Gains:** A simple, visually appealing way to see which politicians and parties are ideologically similar at a single glance.

## Situation Assessment
* **Resources:** Data will be sourced from the OpenParlData API via the `swissparlpy` Python library. We will utilize `sentence-transformers` for generating multilingual embeddings and `UMAP` for dimensionality reduction. Visualizations will be built using Python frameworks (e.g., Matplotlib/Seaborn/Plotly).
* **Constraints & Time:** A draft charter is due March 20, 2026. The final product and documentation must be deployed via GitHub Pages by June 1, 2026.
* **Risks:**
  * High computational cost of generating AI embeddings locally (mitigated by processing data offline and saving it as a static CSV for the dashboard).
  * Multilingual nuances (German, French, Italian) potentially skewing the semantic clustering (mitigated by using a robust multilingual transformer model).

## Visualization Concept
This concept translates the project objectives into a concrete visual format, directly addressing the user pains and gains identified above.

* **Product form:** An interactive dashboard application.
* **Visual encodings:**
  * *Main Graphic:* A 2D Scatter plot. Each dot represents a single speech (mapped via UMAP X/Y coordinates). Color hue encodes the **Party** (Categorical).
  * *Supporting Graphics:* Bar charts encoding average speech duration or average sentiment score by Party (Length/Height encoding).
* **Interactivity:** Dropdown menus to filter the scatter plot by Party, Language, or Canton. Hover tooltips on the dots to reveal the Speaker's Name, Date, and a short snippet of the speech.
* **Narrative and annotation:** A brief introductory text panel explaining how the AI reads the speeches and creates the "semantic map," guiding the user on how to interpret the spatial distances between dots.
* **Target medium and integration:** Hosted online as a standalone web application (e.g., via Streamlit Community Cloud), accessible via a standard web browser.

**Intended Value Dimensions:**
* **Cognitive and analytical value:** The scatter plot clustering instantly reveals hidden patterns of ideological similarity that are impossible to detect by reading raw text [@card1999; @vanwijk2005].
* **Communicative value:** Color-coding by party immediately communicates whether parties act as a unified bloc or if their members' rhetoric is scattered.
* **Experiential and aesthetic value:** Turning dry government transcripts into an interactive, explorable "map" of ideas provides a modern and engaging experience, fostering trust in the data through transparency.

## Project Plan
Below is the preliminary timetable for the visualization project:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-project-plan
%%| fig-cap: Preliminary project plan in the form of a Gantt chart.
gantt
    title Project Plan
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    tickInterval 1week
    
    section Project Understanding
        Context, Stakeholder & User analysis :a1, 2026-03-16, 3d
        Draft Project Charta :milestone, m1, 2026-03-20, 0d
        First Coaching Session :a2, 2026-03-23, 5d
    
    section Data Acquisition & Processing
        API Data Fetching (swissparlpy) :a3, 2026-04-01, 5d
        AI Embeddings & UMAP reduction :a4, 2026-04-06, 7d
        Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) :a5, 2026-04-13, 5d
        Complete Data Report :a6, 2026-04-18, 3d
        
    section Visual Encoding and Design
        Draft Visualization Concept :a7, 2026-04-21, 7d
        Implement Dashboard Prototype :a8, 2026-04-28, 14d
        Refine UI/UX & Interactivity :a9, 2026-05-12, 5d
        
    section Evaluation & Deployment
        User Testing / Peer Review :a10, 2026-05-17, 5d
        Finalize Quarto Documentation :a11, 2026-05-22, 7d
        Final Project Submission :milestone, m2, 2026-06-01, 0d
        Prepare Presentation Slides :a12, 2026-06-02, 7d
```

## Roles and Contact Details
TBD

List the people involved in the development work here with their role titles, tasks and contact details.
